---
title: "How to submit R markdown assignments"
subtitle: "CHC9012 | National Taiwan Normal University"
editor: source
---

# Two options

-  Uploading the Markdown document on Moodle
-  Knit it to a PDF file, and upload the PDF file (recommended)

# How to knit to a PDF file?

## Video overview

You can focus on the second part of the video (starting around 1m30s).

![](CHC9012_HowToSubmitAssignment.mp4)

## Debugging

When you try to knit your R Markdown document into a PDF file, you may encounter this message:

```diff
sh: pdflatex: command not found

Error: LaTeX failed to compile Untitled.tex. See The R package tinytex - Helper Functions to Manage TinyTeX, and Compile LaTeX Documents - Yihui Xie | 谢益辉 for debugging tips. See Untitled.log for more info.
In addition: Warning message:
In system2(..., stdout = if (use_file_stdout()) f1 else FALSE, stderr = f2) :
error in running command
Execution halted
```


This means that you need to install a package before creating the PDF document. Here are the steps:
Run this line of code in the console block: 

```r
tinytex::install_tinytex()
```

It can take some time, up to 5 minutes. Now, try to click one more time on "Knit to PDF". It's supposed to work.
If it still doesn't work, please contact me!
